description_long: This object is a cylindrical electric drill with a robust metallic body, primarily painted in a yellow color with black and metallic accents. The drill features a long, protruding shaft or rod, which appears to be the drill bit or actuator, extending from one end. Both ends of the cylinder have circular eyelets, likely for mounting or attachment purposes. A handle or lever is attached along the side, suggesting manual or mechanical operation. The surface is smooth and hard, with no visible brand markings or ventilation slots. The overall design is industrial and utilitarian, suitable for heavy-duty urban or construction tasks.
description: A yellow, cylindrical electric drill with a long metallic shaft, side handle, and circular mounting eyelets, designed for industrial use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular end with the shaft centered and the handle partially visible on the side.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of the drill, the extended shaft, and the side handle running parallel to the body.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the opposite circular end and the alignment of the shaft and handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the full length, shaft, and handle from the other side.
